description: Physicians today face a significant challenge with documentation, usually spending up to 50% of their time on administrative tasks. Dr. Shiv Rao, a UPMC-trained practicing cardiologist, saw this issue firsthand and found a way to solve this massive pain point. Abridge is building the generative AI layer of healthcare, streamlining care delivery workflows and enhancing patient outcomes without the need for human intervention.

Millions of eligible Americans still struggle to access basic healthcare services due to challenges like booking appointments or finding transportation. We believe Accompany Health is the solution to this dilemma. The core of Accompany Health’s model – aligning business incentives with better patient outcomes – has the potential to revolutionize not just the health but also the lives of patients everywhere.

Organizations of all sizes are moving to the cloud and leveraging open source software as the backbone of their data infrastructure. While larger companies have the resources and expertise to manage these enterprise deployments internally, most companies require some level of third-party support to effectively utilize open source. Aiven democratizes access to the latest open source technologies by offering fully-managed services for popular open source projects like Apache Kafka and Cassandra, Elasticsearch, M3 and PostgreSQL in the public cloud.

With impressive GDP growth and a rapidly expanding middle class, Southeast Asia is fertile ground for a new generation of fintech investors. Yet in Indonesia, investing has long been considered an activity for the rich and privileged, with stock market access limited to all but high net worth individuals. Ajaib is on a mission to change that with a foundational, forward-thinking financial platform that’s bridging the stock penetration rate between Indonesia and the rest of the world. Founders Yada Piyajomkwan and Anderson Sumarli are forging a path to financial flexibility for emerging economies by providing stock market access and education to first-time investors, a group that has traditionally been neglected. Stock trading is just the first of many services Ajaib can provide to this fast-growing demographic, with its mobile-native, technology-first platform offering huge expansion potential for supporting online banking services, FX, crypto trading and more. With greater financial freedom across Southeast Asia in their sights, the Ajaib team is just getting started.

It’s no secret that the U.S. healthcare system suffers from wasteful spending and suboptimal outcomes. Traditionally, the sicker patients get, the more profitable they become for healthcare providers—a system that has long created misaligned incentives by rewarding the treatment of disease rather than its prevention. Through its proprietary technology platform and local, hands-on support teams, Aledade promotes value-based care programs in which compensation is aligned with quality—as opposed to quantity—of care. By partnering with primary care practices to build and lead Accountable Care Organizations (ACOs), the startup is helping deliver better patient care, improved health for overall populations, and reduced care costs. As beneficial to doctors as it is to patients, Aledade’s model is paving the way for a stronger, healthier healthcare system and society as a whole.

Today’s product teams are increasingly recognized as critical drivers of revenue. While the role and prominence of product teams have evolved over the years, the same can’t be said for their toolsets. Forced to use unwieldy or archaic tools—often intended for marketing organizations—product teams are in desperate need of better technology that’s built for their specific needs. That’s where Amplitude comes in. Built explicitly for the product team and CPO, their next-generation analytics platform focuses directly on user behavior to help companies build better products. By leveraging NOVA, its proprietary engine optimized to answer complex queries over aggregated sets of users, the enterprise platform enables product teams to efficiently analyze the information most relevant to their businesses. A solution beloved among cutting-edge start-ups and Fortune 500s alike, Amplitude is a market-defining B2B SaaS company in the emerging category of product analytics software.
A threat intelligence platform that identifies cyber threats and facilitates trusted threat sharing

Anthropic delivers the kind of breakthrough moment that reminds us why we fell in love with technology. Many of us remember the first time we held an iPhone. Using Claude feels like that. Claude has quickly become indispensable for drafting, building, researching, and making sense of complex data. Not because it is novel, but because it is genuinely useful. What makes Anthropic special is the combination: a world-class team that expands the frontier of AI, transforms that capability into elegant products, and brings them to market in a way enterprises trust. We believe Anthropic will be an era-defining company.
leadership: Dario Amodei, Daniela Amodei, Krishna Rao

description: Appcharge is redefining the economics of gaming by giving publishers ownership of their players, their profits, and their paths to growth. With lightning-fast traction, deep customer trust, and a platform that feels invisible yet indispensable, Appcharge is becoming the infrastructure layer for first-party payments. The team has a rare combination of domain fluency and executional intensity required to lead this shift. In an era where power is moving back to creators, Appcharge is the engine behind that movement.
A leader in application intelligence for software-defined business

Attentive’s suite of software tools breathes fresh life into marketing communication by helping top brands build a list of opt-in mobile messaging subscribers, automatically send highly personalized messages, power two-way conversations and measure the impact of each message. Less crowded than other channels and preferred by key demographics, messaging promises higher click-through rates and strong, immediate ROI. By presenting a fundamentally better way to engage, Attentive is creating a new reality of welcome and relevant dialogue between brands and consumers.

People expect personalized, intuitive experiences powered by the latest AI models. But getting these models into production, where they can deliver sustained business value, is notoriously complex. Baseten's inference engine enables companies to easily and securely deploy models in production, saving engineering time by handling complexities like GPU management in the cloud and reducing cold starts. Baseten ensures AI scalability with a focus on both low latency and high reliability.
The first corporate card for startups
The social media management software of choice for the world’s top advertisers
A leading digital news provider focused on Wall Street, Technology and other business verticals

Cape is rebuilding the mobile network stack with privacy at its core by replacing legacy carrier infrastructure with its own secure mobile core and custom SIMs. By decoupling identity from connectivity through anonymous authentication and unlinkable payment, Cape protects users at the network layer, not just the app layer. In a world where mobile infrastructure was never designed for privacy, we believe Cape is defining a new standard for secure connectivity.
The largest online care destination in the world

We spend roughly a third of our lives sleeping, so choosing where to spend those all-important hours may seem like a daunting task. For most of us, buying a mattress is a true investment—one that will last five to seven years or maybe more, and directly impacts our mental and physical health. The decision is personal and intimate, but the traditional retail buying experience can be rife with stressors, from fluorescent showroom lights to pushy salespeople and sub-par delivery options. Luckily, Casper has come along to shake up the stagnant sleep industry, replacing lumpy innerspring coil with lush memory foam and exorbitant markups with direct-to-consumer convenience. Plus, with the help of some sleek making-of animations and charming unboxing videos, Casper’s e-commerce shopping experience is just plain fun. In 2015, just a year after they sold their first mattress, near-universal acclaim and an incredible founding team made the decision to invest easy. Today, as a market-leading brand that’s synonymous with a better night’s sleep, Casper continues to change people’s lives for the better.

We invested in Chainguard because we see its potential to become the default solution for developers to build secure software. Chainguard has created rabid customer love by creating a single destination for hardened versions of popular software containers — the standalone, executable packages that includes everything needed to run a piece of software. Chainguard minimizes attack surfaces and makes building and running applications safer for all companies.
leadership: Dan Lorenc, Matt Moore, Kim Lewandowski, Ville Aikas

description: The leading software solution for background screening and verifications

Software delivery has become a continuous loop. A single workflow brings source code through an automated build to functionality and compatibility testing, and finally to deployment—a process that repeats frequently. As the leading cloud-based platform for the automated building, testing and deployment of new code, CircleCI expedites this process and reduces risk by ensuring that code pushed to production is stable code. Developers can be a very particular bunch, but they have shown their faith in CircleCI with their time, energy and money. No developer wants to push a bug to an end user, and CircleCI helps reduce that risk by ensuring that code pushed to production is stable. By managing and running all of the necessary infrastructure, CircleCI saves precious time and allows developers to focus on releasing the next killer feature.

ClickHouse is the rare database that delivers speed, scale and cost-efficiency without compromise. Its technical edge powers real-time analytics for thousands of customers, but what truly sets it apart is business impact: better products, faster feedback, and happier end users. As data infrastructure evolves to meet AI’s demands, ClickHouse is the platform built to keep up.
leadership: Aaron Katz, Alexey Milovidov, Yury Izrailevsky

description: From nurses to lab assistants, many healthcare professionals in the United States are overworked and underpaid. Meanwhile, hospitals and healthcare facilities are facing a critical shortage in trained personnel that leaves them unable to adequately care for their patients. The COVID-19 pandemic further aggravated the strain on this fragile system, demonstrating the urgent need for technological evolution. As a marketplace that directly connects medical professionals with facilities, Clipboard Health aims to provide more opportunity and flexibility by creating a mutually beneficial system. Nurses gain greater control over their work schedules while healthcare facilities gain on-demand access to trained personnel to fill unpredictable staffing shortages. With growth implications beyond the healthcare industry, Clipboard Health is paving a way to a better future of work.
The world’s most popular way to buy and sell bitcoin, ethereum, and litecoin

The real estate market has long been burdened by painfully slow processes and antiquated technology. Many venture-backed start-ups have tried to transform the industry by replacing real estate agents—and ultimately failed. The founders of Compass, on the other hand, recognize how crucial real estate agents are to the marketplace. By empowering real estate agents with an AI-powered end-to-end platform, Compass solves industry inefficiencies and delivers exceptional service to both buyers and sellers, who are saved from a complex and stressful process. And the agents themselves are able to win more listings, better properties and generate more commission income than they would at a traditional brokerage. We call that a win-win-win.

Creating consistency across engineering resources, services, owners and projects gets harder by the day. But until now, engineering leaders – unlike their peers in sales, marketing, HR or finance – haven't had a centralized, SaaS-based platform to keep them organized, informed and in sync. Cortex changed all that. Cortex makes teams better by providing a lower TCO, faster setup time and higher overall ease-of-use than is possible with Backstage. We consistently hear this kind of high praise for Cortex from IVP portfolio companies. The director of engineering at one of them told us, “Cortex started as a service catalog and now it’s a full developer portal. The ultimate state is for teams to use it on a day-to-day basis to, for example, simplify launching a new service or quickly check ownership for another application component.” Cortex created a powerful, differentiated vision for reducing the toil and the mental load of building and running engineering services so that developers and teams are free to focus on the rewarding work – the building.
leadership: Anish Dhar, Ganesh Datta, Nikhil Unni

description: Cowboy Space Corporation is building for a future in which access to power becomes as important to AI as access to chips. As terrestrial infrastructure struggles to keep pace with demand, Cowboy is taking a vertically integrated approach that combines energy, compute, and launch in a single system designed from first principles. We believe that vision is both ambitious and timely, and that Baiju and team are pursuing a bold new layer of infrastructure with the technical depth and long-term orientation required to make it real.

Cradle is bringing biology into the digital age. Its AI-powered platform empowers scientists to design proteins—the building blocks of life—with precision, turning a once labor-intensive trial-and-error process into a predictive and efficient workflow. With a focus on customer empowerment and data ownership, Cradle’s innovative approach is driving breakthroughs across healthcare, climate, and sustainability. Led by a visionary team, Cradle is redefining what’s possible in bioengineering.
leadership: Stef van Grieken, Jelle Prins, Elise de Reus, Eli Bixby, Harmen van Rossum

description: If cost were no object, most observability teams would store more enterprise data. With Cribl, you don’t need to choose—their suite of products allows teams to store and analyze more data without spending more time and money. Wherever your data comes from, wherever it needs to go, Cribl replaces compromise with freedom and flexibility. In the midst of creating a disruptive new category, Cribl applies logic to streams of data, providing an “observability pipeline” built to route security and machine data to where it has the most value to a business. Cribl offers complete flexibility, allowing customers to choose which data to keep, data format, data store to be sent to, and everything with a full-fidelity copy in low-cost storage. As a critical pillar for observability teams, Cribl allows the humans to focus on what really matters.
The leader in cloud-delivered endpoint protection and cybersecurity
A full-service skincare company offering access to expert dermatologists and personalized treatment plans

With every passing year, enterprises, governments and financial institutions become more concerned with cyber risk—and rightfully so. Despite organizations spending billions of dollars on IT security each year to protect their data and customers, breaches still occur at an alarming rate, underscoring the necessity for cyber insurance as an effective, complementary solution for security. Many insurers, however, fail to adequately measure cyber risk due to lack of understanding, and the majority of policies have low liability limits that don’t begin to cover the potential financial loss of a hack. As the leading economic modeling platform for assessing cyber risk, Cyence has the answer. By leveraging both machine learning and econometric models, Cyence’s technology is able not only to predict the incidence of a cyber event for organizations with impressive accuracy, but also to evaluate the severity, accumulation and exposure of a particular policy. Empowered by reliable information, insurers who use Cyence are taking a massive step forward in the fight against cyber attacks.
The leading decision-making platform for the mobile app economy
The essential monitoring platform for cloud applications
The leader at connecting digital media and offline purchasing data
A real-time information discovery company

As our world becomes more interconnected, demand for high-quality NLP (Natural Language Processing) has never been higher. While consumer solutions can convey the basics, legacy commercial solutions are often inefficient — requiring days or weeks of turnaround time — and can be prohibitively expensive. We believe that’s about to change thanks to DeepL, a revolutionary AI-powered machine translation platform. DeepL instantly translates any written content — whether a short email to a friend in another country, or complex business documents like legal contracts, technical manuals, and virtually any commercial web page — accurately and effortlessly into dozens of different languages.

Hourly workers are the backbone of our economy. In 2017, the Bureau of Labor and Statistics reported hourly workers comprised more than 60% of the workforce in the United States, and on a global scale, the estimate is 80-90%. Still, there is a stark lack of enterprise software tools that directly address these workers’ vital everyday needs. Shift scheduling and other communication are relegated to pen and paper or whiteboards, while time is tracked through punch clocks and basic spreadsheets. Millions of workers are clamoring for modern, easy-to-use SaaS solutions that bring the mundane tasks associated with work and scheduling into the modern world. Thankfully, there’s Deputy. By providing a single, convenient application to handle shift scheduling, timesheets, tasking and communication, Deputy is simplifying the lives of all employees. During diligence, one user literally cried tears of joy telling us she had gained more control over her schedule, enabling her to spend less time commuting for short shifts and more time with her family. Deputy already has a history of success with massive enterprises like McDonald’s and Amazon, but we know they’ve only just scratched the surface of what they can achieve.

What began as a chatting app for gamers is increasingly becoming the go-to platform for communities of all shapes and sizes. The breakout platform’s crystal-clear voice chat, robust security, screen sharing, game integrations and sweet tunes are beloved by a loyal and ever-growing user base, earning the platform some of the best engagement metrics we’ve seen. Attracting everyone from small groups of friends to developers and influencers engaging with massive audiences, Discord is poised to expand its reach and become the global hub for gaming.
A cloud-based executive management platform that gives users direct, real-time access to the information they care about, all in one place

From day one, the mission of Dream Games has been to build an enduring gaming company that delights millions of users around the world with high-quality mobile games. So far, the startup has done just that with Royal Match, a fast-growing casual puzzle title released in March 2021. The Dream Games team is home to some of the brightest minds across product, design, creative and marketing, and their meticulous attention to game mechanics, speed, design and social features has earned them some truly impressive early adoption and retention stats. Dream Games has the perfect mix of visionary expertise to create and scale quality games into beloved, top-grossing titles and long-standing franchises.
A service that lets people bring their documents, photos, and videos everywhere and share them easily
An AI talent intelligence platform with everything you need to hire and develop people to their highest potential
The first automated global Internet intelligence company

Recent years have seen a huge popularity spike in combat sports like boxing and MMA. Families and groups of friends gather around the TV to watch big-time UFC showdowns… and then want to go and learn to throw punches like the pros. Luckily, there’s FightCamp to provide an accessible, at-home boxing workout. The unique strength, cardio and stress-relieving benefits of boxing have made it an extremely sought-after fitness option for the masses. FightCamp technology offers users the best of both worlds—the convenience and comfort of an at-home workout and the motivational atmosphere of an online fitness community. Well on its way to becoming a major household brand, FightCamp is doing its part to create a healthier population. We couldn’t be more excited to be in their corner for the journey ahead.

The world of design, once disconnected and solitary, is now one of real-time collaboration and community—thanks to the online creative tools of design powerhouse Figma. Customer love for their products is unparalleled, from Figma itself to FigJam and Figma Community. Users uniformly praise the ways in which Figma fosters a transparent, community-driven, open source design process. By providing a hyper-convenient digital space for teams to brainstorm and execute together, Figma is making design fun again. We’re honored to accompany the exceptional team at Figma on their continued journey of excellence. Much like design, the best B2B SaaS companies are built through a process of enduring collaboration.
The market leader in SaaS based GPS tracking applications for local commercial fleets

Obesity is one of the leading causes of preventable death in the United States, and by 2030, nearly half of the population will be clinically obese. Society tells us that if we eat less and exercise more, we’ll lose weight and enjoy a better quality of life, but when we don’t find success, we’re shamed for our lack of willpower. In reality, it’s not that simple; medical research reveals a wide range of biological, psychological and environmental factors play a role in weight loss. Obesity is a disease, not a decision, and it needs to be treated like one. Found, a digital-first weight care company, delivers a comprehensive wellness program of evaluation, medication and lifestyle coaching that breaks down the stigmas surrounding weight to help its members change their relationships with their bodies. Powered by expert-led clinical research, this breakout company has helped tens of thousands keep up with their grandchildren, fit into their wedding rings again and find healthier and more fulfilled versions of themselves. Finally, democratized integrated weight care has been Found.

Consumers are overloaded with reviews. Want to know what your fellow humans think of a hotel, restaurant, app, service, or particular brand of laundry detergent? Thousands of opinions are just a click away on Google, Yelp, Amazon and more. On the flip side, it’s nearly impossible to find honest, helpful information about products for your business. The purchasing process is mind-numbing, rife with sleazy salespeople, opaque pricing data and the inability to easily compare alternative solutions. The answer is G2. With over 2 million peer reviews, G2 brings authenticity and transparency to B2B technology purchasing, giving buyers the confidence they need to select the best solutions for their unique needs. In addition to empowering businesses, G2 is also helping the IT market as a whole become more efficient by preventing purchases of “shelfware,” software that goes unused because it doesn’t actually address the issue at hand. We’re thrilled to invest in and work with G2 to break down barriers and provide companies with the tools they need to cut through the noise.

Presentations are not a formatting problem. They are a storytelling problem. As work has shifted to faster, more distributed, and more visual modes of communication, the cost of turning ideas into clear narratives has continued to rise. Gamma rethinks presentations from first principles as an AI-native platform that turns raw thinking into polished storytelling without the drag of design busywork. What sets Gamma apart is not just speed, but taste, abstraction, and extensibility. With agents, APIs, and team workflows, Gamma is becoming a system of record for professional storytelling, not just a better deck tool.

The future of logistics and the physical economy will be built on ground truth. Systems that rely on self-reported or delayed data will continue to break down as supply chains face rising volatility, fraud, and margin pressure. GenLogs is creating a new visibility layer by observing trucks in real time, producing intelligence that becomes more valuable and more embedded as adoption grows. With a network that compounds and a team purpose-built to verify reality at scale, GenLogs is positioned to become foundational infrastructure for how goods move across the country.
leadership: Ryan Joyce, Blake Balch, Joe Sherman

description: The first and largest GIF search engine making expression a little more moving
The world’s largest software community where developers can easily discover, reuse and contribute to projects using an industry standard workflow

In every company, the quest to find information—from emails and contracts to Slack threads and code snippets—becomes a time-consuming ordeal across many applications, a frustrating and antiquated process that hinders productivity. Glean is the enterprise AI assistant that will revolutionize business productivity, making the task of locating and sharing any piece of information as simple as finding an obscure song lyric, transforming this hunt into an efficient and streamlined process.

Beauty and skincare brand Glossier hatched out of Emily Weiss’s blog Into The Gloss, famous for The Top Shelf, a series of interviews with beauty editors, celebrities, artists, business leaders and more about their skincare routines and favorite products. To this day, the site is a gold mine of market research that fuels the Glossier line. What products do makeup enthusiasts swear by? What products do they wish existed but don’t? While other retailers only hear from their customers through focus groups and outsourced surveys, Glossier is creating a direct line to its community, which means products are always a hit and never a miss. Competitors may seek to create a following on social media, but Glossier was born there, and is meeting customers right where they’re at—on their phones.
An AI-powered communication assistant that helps users make their writing more impactful
leadership: Brad Hoover, Alex Shevchenko, Max Lytvyn

description: The leading global technology platform driving in-store visits and sales by leveraging location as the primary source of intent
The number one publisher on mobile with the largest mobile audience in the U.S.

In the world of life sciences, identifying and connecting with the right healthcare professionals is a crucial part of the drug discovery process. Sadly, data is notoriously fragmented, opaque and siloed in the healthcare industry—it’s typically impossible to see an HCP’s entire background, including publications, clinical trials and conferences, on a single platform. Or it was until H1 came along. H1 is the most comprehensive platform of its kind, aggregating real-time data on over 9 million HCPs across 70 countries, 700 medical societies, 16,000 healthcare organizations, 350,000 clinical trials and 160 million peer-reviewed publications. By connecting all stakeholders in the healthcare ecosystem, the medtech startup enables greater collaboration and discovery. With H1’s critical information at their fingertips, teams in the life sciences industry can accelerate the development of medical devices and therapeutics that will save countless lives.

A decade ago, developers built software applications with a monolithic approach, releasing their code perhaps every six or twelve months. Today, tech giants have primed consumers to expect rapid updates and constant access to the latest technologies. The capability to increase the pace of delivery, sometimes by a thousandfold, has become a strategic advantage for tech-forward companies—and a disadvantage for enterprises that can’t keep up with consumer expectation. Harness, the industry’s first software delivery platform to use AI for simplifying DevOps processes, is democratizing continuous delivery by providing a freemium SaaS solution for automation. It also enables software engineering teams to deliver code to customer-facing applications more efficiently and securely, improving the quality of their software in production. Harness is perfectly positioned to help its B2B customers better understand, manage and automate the evolving software lifecycle, and we’re excited to have the opportunity to invest in and support their team on a remarkable journey.

Long before it was the norm, HashiCorp’s software enabled countless enterprises to adopt a cloud operating model. From Autodesk to Goldman Sachs, customers absolutely loved the consistent workflows to provision, secure, connect and run any infrastructure for any application. Today, multi-cloud environments are the dominant form of commercial digital operations, and HashiCorp’s market-leading open-source products continue to be among the most downloaded on the planet. Their flagship product, the HashiCorp Cloud Platform (HCP), presents practitioners with fully managed infrastructure from a central point, allowing companies to build cloud-native applications that can run anywhere. Past, present and future—HashiCorp defines the curve for the cloud computing era.

We all know that a healthy heart is central to overall good health, yet heart disease remains a leading cause of death in the U.S., claiming millions of lives each year. Hello Heart is on a mission to change that. As an AI company focused exclusively on heart health, Hello Heart has built a platform that predicts and prevents cardiac events before they happen, identifying risk earlier and more precisely than traditional clinical models. From the company's earliest days, Maayan Cohen and the team embedded AI at the core of the platform. As the cardiac prevention partner to a majority of large U.S. health plans and hundreds of public and private employers, Hello Heart is reducing both the rate of chronic heart conditions and the overall cost of care. It's rare to be able to invest in a company providing such a vital and virtuous service.

Hims & Hers is a fast-growing company that thinks and acts differently. Their multi-specialty telehealth platform connects consumers with licensed healthcare professionals directly, providing greater accessibility and giving users greater control over their own healthcare. By challenging stigmas around physical and mental health conditions and opening up access to safe, quality healthcare, they are reimagining primary care in a rapidly changing world. Intentional about building a public-ready company from the beginning, the team at Hims & Hers has also defied stereotypes about how start-ups should grow and set themselves up for long-term success—we’re just proud to be on this journey that’s sure to last long into the future!
The world’s leading online marketplace of vacation rentals

The average full-time employee in the United States spends 8.81 hours working every day, much more than the amount we spend on personal care, friends and family, and sleep. As a result, many of us expect our professional experiences to bring us purpose, structure, learning, achievement, joy and fulfillment. For the lucky ones that have scored their “dream job,” that may be the case—but what about the rest of us? During his time as Google’s first Senior Vice President of People Operations, Laszlo Bock completely transformed the company’s culture, relying on a combination of organizational behavioral theory and data-driven approach. With the goal of bringing greater satisfaction to the rest of the working world, Bock founded Humu together with other Google alums Wayne Crosby and Jessie Wisdom. The behavioral change software company helps people increase productivity and happiness through proprietary technology rooted in science and machine learning. Like Humu, we believe that work can, and should, be better for everyone, and we couldn’t be more excited to be investing and partnering with their breakout wellness company to bring this vision to reality in the years to come.
leadership: Laszlo Bock, Wayne Crosby, Jessie Wisdom

description: The stock exchange that believes that every investor has the right to trade on equal and fair terms, on every trade

Since its founding in 2008, Indiegogo has had explosive success as the world’s largest and fastest-growing crowdfunding platform, and we don’t see the momentum slowing down. In contrast to other crowdfunding vendors, Indiegogo is on a mission to “democratize funding” by providing an open platform and flexible funding model that allows anyone to participate. Still in its infancy, the crowdfunding industry is poised to become a massive sector, and we see this pioneering company leading the charge.

Brothers Brent and Brad Handler of the private luxury travel club Exclusive Resorts launched Inspirato as a technology-driven travel company that could grow even faster, deliver luxury vacations to an even larger number of travelers and truly disrupt the $135 billion luxury travel market. Inspirato provides exceptional vacations for affluent travelers through an innovative subscription platform while ensuring attractive economics for their real estate and hospitality partners around the world. Instead of spending hours researching and planning a vacation only to discover lackluster accommodations and poor service upon arrival, travelers can now rely on Inspirato to deliver unforgettable experiences that deepen relationships and create enduring memories. We’re certain the now-public company will enjoy a bright future of remarkable growth for years to come.

Jasper is a next-generation communications platform that makes generative artificial intelligence accessible to businesses and individual creators, enabling them to break through writer’s block, create original art, and repackage content for format, language and tone. Since its launch in January 2021, Jasper has earned a passionate following among people who have saved countless hours creating and formatting written and visual content on thousands of subjects. In minutes, Jasper can write a 1,500-word blog post, emails for a targeted marketing campaign or multiple scroll-stopping headlines for an ecommerce store’s Instagram ads. We believe Jasper is among the next wave of breakout, AI-assisted platforms that will transform how people work and live.
leadership: Chris Hull, Dave Rogenmoser, JP Morgan

description: Markets turn belief into consequence. Kalshi makes conviction tradable—transforming real‑world events into a regulated asset class on a CFTC‑registered exchange—and is building the everything exchange we back: a trusted, compliant market where anyone can invest in what they believe.

Design is everywhere. It influences how we think, defines culture and shapes how we interact with and perceive the world around us. Somewhere along the way, graphic designers got left behind. The creative people, whose business is to create logos, assets and images (the foundations of great design), are stuck using complex, desktop-based tools from the 1980s. Kittl brings visual design into the age of AI. The customer-loved platform enables anyone to create sophisticated designs, with pixel-perfect accuracy, regardless of background or experience level.
One of the leading independent European online payments providers

LangChain is building the foundational infrastructure for the multi-model AI era. As agents become the standard interface for enterprise software, LangChain has emerged as the trusted, open-source platform developers rely on to build, evaluate, and deploy them. LangChain is defining the category of agent engineering and powering the next generation of intelligent applications.

Laurel turns the most painful process in professional services, time tracking, into a source of truth, insight, and margin. Its personalized AI models reconstruct time with context and clarity, helping firms capture revenue, cut overhead, and give professionals their time back. We believe Laurel is becoming the system of record for how high-performing teams work.

In 1999, LegalZoom founders and former lawyers Brian Liu and Brian Lee set out to democratize law by making fundamental legal resources available to everyone on the Internet. Beginning with basic forms and documents, the early-aughts start-up quickly moved toward regulatory compliance, company formation, wills, and estate planning. More than two decades later, LegalZoom has established the brand recognition and a superior customer experience that can only be built by patience and persistence. LegalZoom is the market leader, offering automation for legal tasks and compliance as well as referrals to pre-screened attorneys for customized legal advice. With over $400 million in revenue, consistent profitability and strong margins, LegalZoom has a bright future as a successful public company.
An industry leader in identity theft protection
A transportation company that makes micromobility affordable and accessible
leadership: Wayne Ting, Toby Sun, Brad Bao

description: A complete business intelligence and data analytics platform that offers real-time insights to drive better decision-making

Lulus is a digital-native apparel brand that has excelled at cultivating direct relationships with its consumers. The team at Lulus goes above and beyond to understand shoppers, which means they know exactly how best to serve and delight them. With support from top-notch design and supply chain teams that create beautiful styles, Lulus provides a devoted social media following with affordable but luxurious apparel. From a retail brick and mortar in Northern California to one of the most beloved e-commerce clothing brands with hundreds of million in revenue, Lulus is sure to reach even more impressive heights in the seasons to come.

Manufacturing runs on precision, yet quality control remains slow, expensive and unreliable for many industries. Lumafield is changing that by making advanced inspection technology faster, more accessible and more effective. By helping companies catch issues early and improve production, Lumafield is transforming how products are built—reducing waste, lowering costs, and increasing reliability. We believe their technology will become a cornerstone of modern manufacturing.
leadership: Eduardo Torrealba, Andreas Bastian, Kevin Cedrone, Ric Fulop, and Scott Johnston

description: Whether we deal with stress, live with a condition like depression, or know someone who does, mental health affects us all. In the U.S., approximately 50 million people suffer from a diagnosable mental health condition, yet only one in seven receive effective treatment. Wellness solutions are hard to find and time-intensive with long waitlists to start. And untreated symptoms tend to intensify, making things worse for individuals, their loved ones and their companies. Enter Lyra. Lyra works with employers to provide immediate access to outstanding and affordable mental health care. About 83% of Lyra members see significant improvement or complete recovery, versus 18% in traditional Employee Assistance Programs. As a result, the startup has seen industry-leading engagement rates, with 7x more employees receiving effective care versus the status quo. Lyra is building something truly special with an offering that hits the mark when it comes to connecting individuals with frictionless mental health care that works.
The market-leading revenue performance management platform powering marketing and sales success

Groundbreaking edtech platform MasterClass turns the world’s leaders into your personal tutor. Learn how to serve aces from Serena Williams, the only tennis player to ever win a Career Golden Slam. Or maybe you’d prefer to perfect a monologue with help from Dame Helen Mirren? If cooking is your thing, Gordon Ramsay has you covered—and you may even get a free lesson on spicy language thrown in. MasterClass has leveraged the advent of online learning to share the expertise of these icons with the world in a rich experiential format that can live indefinitely. And though the content alone is world-class, MasterClass offers even more in the form of a stellar online community. Students, enthusiasts and experts share learning and insights with one another, furthering not only their own experience, but also that of the instructors, many of whom have cited the opportunity as their most rewarding professional experience. We are proud to play a role in making the knowledge of the masters accessible to everyone, forever.
The largest global provider of online business management software to the beauty, health and wellness industry

Data observability company Monte Carlo was built to counteract the alarming phenomenon of “data downtime”—periods of time when data is inexplicably missing or inaccurate. While breakages and errors are a natural and unavoidable result of increasing data complexity, the universal and costly problem of untrustworthy data was one that required solving. In response, Monte Carlo carved out a new category for themselves and set out to help all organizations reduce data downtime, deliver data reliability and ultimately become data-driven with their data observability platform. This disruptive company provides an absolutely necessary solution—for us, it was a matter of when, not if, to invest.

Motive provides a modern fleet management SaaS solution that handles compliance, safety, fleet tracking, fuel tax reporting, maintenance and route optimization on one platform. Other fleet management solutions are expensive, antiquated and have poor UX, but Motive, formerly KeepTruckin, has harnessed the power of the smartphone to build a driver-first logging and fleet management tool. Over 120,000 companies, from small businesses to Fortune 500 enterprises, rely on Motive to transform the safety, productivity, and profitability of their related transportation operations. We are honored to partner with them as they continue to pave the way forward for the third generation of fleet management software.
A provider of a software platform that enables organizations to create a network of applications, data sources and devices

Founded in 2009 during the Great Recession with just $800 in capital, NerdWallet is now one of the largest and most important digital resources for both personal and small business financial information and education. From the get-go, the NerdWallet team’s strategy has been to focus on building long-term trust instead of capitalizing on short-term revenue opportunities. This kind of philosophy might be considered a “risk”—but it’s also why we decided to invest in NerdWallet. Their dedication to their audience and the quality of their fintech product has ensured that they’re the most reliable source for info on everything from credit cards, personal loans, student loans, insurance and mortgages to banking, small business loans, investing, travel finances and more. The NerdWallet mission is to empower as many people as possible with financial knowledge—and there’s nothing short-term about that.
The world's leading Internet subscription service for enjoying movies and TV shows

Neighbors in more than 300,000 neighborhoods across 11 countries rely on Nextdoor, the social media and networking service where communities come together to greet newcomers, exchange recommendations and read the latest local news. Where neighbors support local businesses, get updates from public agencies, borrow garden tools and sell furniture. Above all, Nextdoor helps you build real-world connections with those nearby, and over one in three U.S. households have put their trust in Nextdoor, making it a top-five online consumer tech product. It’s a beautiful day in the neighborhood.
A marketing technology company delivering products ambitious companies use and rely on to grow their businesses
Founded 2008 Partnered 2009 Acquired 2010

Niantic is the worldwide leader in mobile augmented reality and gaming experiences. Known for developing the smash hit Pokémon GO in collaboration with The Pokémon Company, Niantic is largely responsible for spearheading the popularity of AR. From imaginative mobile games to other exciting products like an AR experience-development platform and revolutionary mapping apps, Niantic is bringing people together through experiences that marry advanced technology with the real world. While the company has made massive strides already, we believe Niantic has just scratched the surface of augmented reality’s immersive potential, and will continue to push boundaries and exceed expectations.

By leveraging AI to automate tedious manual tasks, Numeric transforms how accountants manage their workflows, allowing them to shift from data entry to strategic decision-making. The explosive growth and enthusiastic feedback from customers underscore the profound impact Numeric is having on the industry. Numeric is not just improving close automation—it's redefining the future of financial operations.
leadership: Parker Gilbert, Andrew Bihl, Anthony Alvernaz

description: The cybersecurity space has been experiencing a paradigm shift. With more and more organizations entrusting SaaS applications with highly sensitive business data, business owners are realizing that protecting sensitive SaaS data is an increasingly critical need. Attacks against this data are dynamic, affecting both third-party integrations and cross-application connections. Unfortunately, security teams often lack the tools, time, resources and experience to confidently protect their organization’s critical services. Thankfully, Obsidian Security has entered the fray to tackle this urgent situation. Obsidian is the first truly comprehensive security and compliance solution built with the express aim of protecting SaaS applications. By analyzing state and activity data within and across business-critical applications, Obsidian helps enterprise security teams manage privilege and access, harden configurations, and identify compromised accounts. Obsidian’s holistic approach to SaaS security goes beyond typical authentication and proxies, providing diagnostic results and actionable recommendations within minutes. Though it’s already had a monumental impact on the security landscape, we can’t wait to discover the other ways in which this fast-growing startup will change the world of SaaS.
A leading online platform for small business lending
A mission-driven, high growth financial services company serving the credit needs of the growing Hispanic market

Papaya is bringing global employment into the AI era. Its AI‑first operating system unifies Workforce OS, Payments OS, and Contingent OS so enterprises can manage employees and contractors, ensure compliance, and run instant cross‑border payments from a single platform. As workforces become more distributed and regulations more complex, Papaya’s blend of real‑time intelligence and human‑backed expertise across 180 countries positions it as the default infrastructure for a borderless global workforce.

Throughout his teaching career, Phil Cutler noticed that students who sought after-school tutoring services typically came from affluent families, while many of those who struggled the most could not afford extracurricular help. In an effort to combat this inequity, Phil founded Paper to improve access and outcomes for all students regardless of any barriers they might face, including socio-economic, geographic, linguistic and more. Paper is now the leading B2B educational support system and provider of high-dosage tutoring for K-12 school districts across North America. Purchased at the district or site level, Paper ensures that support for students is truly equitable, scalable and cost-effective. The fast-growing edtech platform provides unlimited one-on-one support 24/7, and offers both live and asynchronous tutoring in 200+ subject areas in four languages. Districts that have adopted Paper are receiving rave reviews from students, teachers and parents—with some students even crediting Paper with their successful admission to college. We are thrilled to partner with Paper to improve educational access in schools everywhere.

We have entered a new era of discovering information online. When we input a question or a phrase into a search bar, we have been conditioned to expect ten blue links: links to other web pages where we can then navigate to find the answer. Now that our eyes have been opened to an alternative – answers written in prose, accurately and comprehensively – we will never look back. Perplexity helps users discover information online by summarizing complex sources into concise, digestible answers - bolstered by supportive citations and custom follow-up questions to help clarify the results.
leadership: Aravind Srinivas, Denis Yarats, Johnny Ho

description: Bringing people and tech together to improve everyone’s financial lives

Business growth is a function of planning and forecasting—financial, resource, product, sales, you name it. And yet, the tools organizations typically use to forge their futures are based on clunky, outdated tech. Excel and Google Sheets simply don’t provide the features modern finance teams need to build complex and highly adaptable models. On the other hand, incumbent enterprise solutions are powerful but require significant IT overhead, training and user patience. In true contrast stands Pigment, a powerful integrated SaaS platform that vastly improves the financial planning process by focusing on ease of use, short time to value, real-time collaboration, expansive breadth of integrations and outstanding customer support. Pigment presents its impressive features via intuitive dashboards and a visually rewarding graphical user experience that customers love. We’re excited to help this breakthrough company grow into an industry-leading fintech platform that helps companies revolutionize decision-making, visualize complex scenarios, and update them in real time, with a powerful, scalable data engine and an intuitive UI.
The pioneer in voice-fraud prevention and authentication

In recent years, we’ve seen a massive increase in e-commerce businesses, with industries like retail and electronics heavily impacted by online distribution channels. Yet a large segment of transactions—car repairs, home services, healthcare visits, and so on—can only be done in person. In fact, according to data from the U.S. Census Bureau, sales from offline retailers comprised 91% of total U.S. commerce in 2017. These offline establishments, however, have largely been left behind when it comes to the adoption of consumer-facing technology and software. Podium is bridging that gap by offering a communication and messaging system geared toward local businesses and their customers. The comprehensive SaaS platform helps businesses manage all communication, from reviews and website conversations to text marketing and payments. Now, brick and mortar businesses can communicate with customers the way customers prefer—using SMS, webchat, Facebook Messenger, Google and Apple business chat—all from one center of operations. As consumers move toward more convenient and often mobile-native modes of communication, Podium will continue to serve local businesses who need to adapt and meet their customers where they are.

CIOs managing SaaS applications are faced with a never-ending slew of questions related to the efficient usage of software. How many apps are we paying for and how much are we spending on each one? Are we using all of our Salesforce licenses? Why are we paying for three different file storage vendors? When your company is using over a hundred different SaaS applications, that’s a lot of data to keep track of, let alone manage effectively. But Productiv surfaces these insights instantly. By analyzing a huge volume of data—login, contract, org chart, expense, usage and more—Productiv helps customers visualize, rationalize and maximize SaaS application spend and usage. Productiv will tell you which applications are collecting dust on the shelf and can be safely done away with, and can even automatically upgrade or downgrade individual employees’ licenses according to their usage needs. The platform is providing a critical service to CIOs, who see immediate value from procurement and security to IT automation and employee enablement.

Since their founding in 2005, Prosper has been revolutionizing the traditional banking and financial services industry as the fastest-growing peer-to-peer lending company in the United States. With a whole suite of smart, simple tools for borrowing, saving and earning, Prosper is on a mission to make a stodgy, manual process faster, easier, cheaper and more efficient. They have consistently delivered innovative products and features that advance this mission and make borrowing better. We are proud to invest in and partner with this forward-thinking fintech company as they lead the transformation of an industry.
A leading independent solid-state array vendor enabling the broad deployment of flash in the data center

In the distributed world of work, employees often feel disconnected from their fellow team members and miss the serendipity, creativity and energy that come from in-person interactions. Monthly or quarterly team bonding activities are, for many remote employees, a poor substitute for the boost they get from daily or weekly office culture. From drawn-out messaging conversations to the pressure of video calls, online communication can be severely fatiguing. Roam is a cloud-based collaboration platform that can be customized to meet the needs of all distributed teams, so people can meet and truly connect regardless of where they are. The Roam Cloud HQ provides a unique visualization of an organization’s workspace—the floor plan can be designed just like a real-world office, with a bird’s eye view of whose “office” is where, who’s in, who’s out, whose door is locked, and who’s in a meeting room with others. Roam aims to reduce the friction of online interaction by inviting personality, visibility and individual connectivity to distributed work. We’ve seen the power of this breakout platform firsthand and are excited to experience how Roam will continue to disrupt a new era of working.

Robinhood is a generation-defining financial services company with the mission to democratize the financial system for everyone, regardless of socioeconomic background. A simple user interface, seamless onboarding and ongoing education make it easy for users to trade commission-free, from stocks and ETFs to cryptocurrencies and IRAs. With its accessible mobile-first products, Robinhood is helping redefine the investment landscape, including its demographics—first-time investors, women, and people of color are all rapidly growing segments of Robinhood’s user base. We’re proud to support Robinhood in providing the next generation of investors with the tools they need to harness their financial future.

Rubrik is a tech company that is revolutionizing how enterprises manage, recover and secure their data across private and public clouds. Traditional methods to manage both the rapid growth and increasing complexity of data are neither sufficient nor economical, but with the world’s first cloud-native data management platform for hybrid and multi-cloud enterprises, Rubrik allows IT organizations to fully realize the flexibility and cost advantages offered by the cloud. Rubrik’s SaaS solutions transcend legacy products by going beyond simple backup capabilities to keep data secure, monitor data risk and quickly recover data. We are proud to stand by Rubrik’s side as they continue to grow and disrupt the massive and antiquated data management market.

Caregiving is one of the most challenging and essential jobs, yet it remains burdened by outdated tools and inefficiencies. Sage is transforming elder care with an integrated platform that uses modern technology to provide faster response times, reduce caregiver burnout and deliver transparency for families. By improving the lives of residents, caregivers and management alike, Sage is setting a new standard for senior living operations and addressing a critical need in a rapidly growing market.
leadership: Raj Mehra, Ellen Johnston, Matthew Lynch

description: As the percentage of consumer activity that occurs online increases, so too does fraud. According to Veriff, online fraudulent activity increased 61% in 2021 alone. SEON is a next-gen fraud prevention platform that helps companies of all sizes completely eliminate fraudulent activity. By leveraging real-time data aggregation to establish a user’s digital footprint, SEON prevents fraudulent account creation and monitors ongoing account activity. SEON offers the most advanced cybersecurity solution for fraud prevention on the market, making them the obvious choice for many of the world’s fastest-growing fintech, gaming and consumer internet companies.
The leading U.S. drone manufacturer and world leader in autonomous flight
A team messaging platform brings all communication together and creates a single, searchable archive

There are billions of mobile users on the planet, and smartphones and tablets are quickly replacing PCs as the dominant computing platform. Snapchat is purpose-built for the mobile platform and offers a unique form of connection with temporary photos or videos that stir a sense of excitement and urgency. Consumers have taken notice. Since its launch in 2011, Snapchat has amassed a very large user base within a valuable demographic, Millennials and Gen Z, and engagement metrics are off the charts. Best of all, Snapchat is just getting started. As the fast-growing social network expands, as more friends and acquaintances sign up, the application becomes increasingly valuable to each of its users. We believe it could grow into one of the most successful consumer applications to date.

The rising tuition costs of higher education are leaving graduates under crushing debt loads—student debt in the United States has surpassed credit card debt as the largest source of consumer debt excluding mortgages. To make matters worse, these debt-ridden graduates are often denied attractive credit from traditional financial institutions because of the firms’ strict adherence to lending policies defined by outdated risk models. SoFi is revolutionizing consumer lending for recent graduates. By providing an alternative risk model based on merit, employee history and other factors, the fintech company is able to save graduates with high credit potential an average of $12,000 by refinancing their existing loans. Members often come to SoFi seeking student loan help, but gain access to an entire platform of products, from mortgages to personal loans, so they can pursue whatever matters most to them, whether that’s a wedding, home ownership or something else entirely. We’re proud to accompany SoFi on their journey to becoming the de facto standard for credit and wealth management for early career professionals.

Healthcare works best when patients are empowered with clear guidance and relentless support, especially in a system as complex and fragmented as ours. Solace Health addresses a foundational gap by making healthcare advocacy accessible by default, not as a privilege reserved for the few who know how to navigate the system. The team’s deeply personal understanding of the patient journey, combined with a model aligned across patients, providers and payors, positions Solace to drive better outcomes at scale. This is the kind of durable infrastructure healthcare has long needed.

With over three billion fans around the world, football (or soccer in the United States) is the largest sport on the planet—and it’s only getting bigger. As football’s popularity continues to rise, fans are looking for more opportunities to engage with the players and teams they love. Sorare, the leader in global fantasy football powered by NFTs, is providing that experience. The platform allows users to collect and trade licensed cards of their favorite players with guaranteed scarcity, construct a fantasy line-up and win cryptocurrency and other prizes based on real-world performance. Powered by NFTs on the Ethereum blockchain, Sorare has been offering football NFTs since 2019 and was one of the first movers in the space. The football world has already thrown its weight behind the fast-growing startup, which has licensing agreements with over 180 global professional clubs. With the NBA and MLB now also on the docket, Sorare is well on its way to becoming one of the largest gaming companies on the planet.

Since its founding in 2007, SoundCloud has experienced explosive growth and become a cultural phenomenon. Today, SoundCloud is the largest online community of artists, bands, DJs and audio creators, and continues to disrupt the traditional media space. From the unknown and avant-garde to the world’s most prominent musicians, comedians, podcasters and more, creators are able to engage with fans in a way completely unique to SoundCloud. Music and other audio media have always been an inherently social experience, and SoundCloud’s social connectivity is a core element of the veteran platform. SoundCloud is truly setting the pace for the future of streaming and entertainment at large.

SteelBrick is a software company dedicated to automating the sales process and boosting revenue. Natively built on Salesforce, the world’s leading CRM platform, SteelBrick simplifies and accelerates the sales process by automatically configuring product bundles, pricing and discounting products correctly and generating a standardized quote. Once the configure-price-quote (CPQ) process is complete, SteelBrick seamlessly creates a contract and invoices the client, leading to faster cash collections. The entire quote-to-cash (QTC) process is a critical step between an enterprise’s CRM and its financial accounting or ERP system, and Salesforce recognized the immense value of SteelBrick’s breakthrough technology early on, moving to acquire it in 2015. We’re proud to be involved in the building of a category-defining, top software company like SteelBrick.

Email remains the backbone of business communication—and one of its most persistent vulnerabilities. Sublime Security is redefining the standard for email security with an AI-powered, customizable platform that adapts to evolving threats. Led by a team with deep domain expertise and a relentless focus on innovation, Sublime empowers organizations to stay ahead of attackers with precision and control. We believe their approach will set the foundation for the future of secure, adaptable communication in an AI-driven world.
The industry’s leading, secure, and purpose-built cloud-based machine data analytics service

Music has always been one of the few creative acts most people experience only as observers. The tools required skill, the barriers were real, and creation remained the province of a talented few. Suno changes that — making music creation as natural and accessible as any other form of self-expression. What Suno is building is not a better music tool. It is an entirely new consumer behavior: one where the act of making a song becomes as meaningful as listening to one.
A leading global mobile games company

Email is fundamental to how we communicate. But if you ask people about their inboxes, they’ll probably tell you email is a necessary evil, a flawed experience and a source of anxiety. While productivity software has improved in leaps and bounds since its introduction, email clients have barely changed—until Superhuman. Superhuman is an email client that aims to help you become happier, more productive and closer to reaching your potential. A lofty goal perhaps, but one that Superhuman is having no trouble delivering on, according to rave reviews from customers. The platform prioritizes speed, AI integration and a delightful UX to help you get through your inbox twice as fast as before, reply to important emails sooner and even achieve the fabled “Inbox Zero” for the first time in years. Superhuman is a special SaaS company with a bold vision for the future of work, fundamentally re-thinking how individuals and teams collaborate.
Superpowers, everywhere you work. Mail, Docs, and AI that work in every app and tab.

Supermetrics, the world leader in data integration tools for marketers, was conceived as the solution to repetitive, soul-crushing work of downloading and cleaning marketing data for analysis. It started as a simple integration between the Google Analytics API and Excel, and has blossomed into a top platform with extensive reach across data sources and destinations. Trusted by 17,000 businesses worldwide, Supermetrics is streamlining data analysis and reporting, empowering marketing teams to take control of the process themselves. Supermetrics is a shining example of how next-gen technology can make every day simpler, and their future is even brighter than their past.

The world runs on credit. Credit allows restaurant owners to open for business, families to buy houses, or students to pay for college. And yet, for many, building credit is impossible. The traditional financial system in emerging markets can’t serve individuals who have little identity information or credit history. Local lenders sometimes provide capital, but often charge exorbitant rates or penalties that further a cycle of stagnation. Currently operating in Mexico, Kenya, India and the Philippines, Tala's fintech platform exists to provide affordable credit to those who have little or no access to capital. Using more than 10,000 mobile data points per customer, Tala underwrites an individual’s creditworthiness and creates a score where it previously didn’t exist. With this score, the startup provides financial access, choice and control to underserved people. Tala’s team aims to expand to address unmet needs for credit worldwide, creating financial opportunities for disadvantaged communities everywhere.

Tanium is both a systems management and security solution. Its single platform delivers complete, accurate and real-time endpoint data, regardless of scale and complexity. Tanium makes it radically easier and faster for systems and security personnel to manage (i.e., install new software or deploy a patch) and protect (i.e., detect malware or respond to an attack) endpoints. With an approach to cybersecurity that’s differentiated from the competition, Tanium has an immediate ROI and as a result is being adopted by the world’s largest organizations extraordinarily quickly. We’ve been thrilled to be a part of investing in and building this unique, category-defining tech company, and we can’t wait to see how they continue to make today’s IT challenges a thing of the past.
Tax reporting requirements make TaxBit a ‘must have’ for both crypto-native companies as well as traditional financial services companies as they increasingly embrace crypto.

Tennr is building the infrastructure to bring speed, transparency, and reliability to the most overlooked part of U.S. healthcare: the referral handoff. Rather than force providers to change their systems, Tennr integrates directly into the workflows they already use — including fax. With a purpose-built AI model and a growing network of connected providers, Tennr is turning back-office automation into a front-line advantage, accelerating care and creating the foundation for a more connected healthcare system.
leadership: Trey Holterman, Diego Baugh, Tyler Johnson

description: Jessica Alba founded The Honest Company in 2011 with the aim to create household products that put human health and the environment first, something she had found the market to be sorely lacking. The company’s name reflects its essence and mission—to create a category-defining brand that embraces and embodies consciousness, transparency and compassion. With in-house labs and an R&D team that performs rigorous research and testing, The Honest Company has brought safe and clean home, beauty and baby to millions of consumers around the world. We’ve been proud to partner with the Honest team as it has pioneered the clean lifestyle movement with beneficial products that don’t compromise effectiveness. We are excited to watch The Honest Company continue to disrupt the consumer packaged goods industry, while always keeping safety, integrity and sustainability top of mind.

The Players’ Tribune is a digital-first media company dedicated to empowering world-class athletes to share their stories with fans. Since launching in 2014, the platform has been the first to break some of the biggest stories in the world of sports thanks to its deep, intimate connection with the athlete community. With a network that includes greats from baseball, football, basketball and beyond, TPT serves as a reliable outlet in a world where narratives are often intentionally distorted and dominated by talking heads whose sole purpose is to scream louder than yesterday. In contrast, TPT offers thoughtful, high-quality and authentic content straight from the source. As a result of the trust it has built among both athletes and fans, The Players’ Tribune is becoming closer to the heartbeat of the game than nearly anyone else.
A behavior change media and technology company helping individuals, companies and communities improve well-being and performance

Application programming interfaces (APIs) enable communication between software, forming an invisible backbone that connects millions of applications online. While critical to millions of businesses, APIs are also especially vulnerable to cybersecurity attacks. Unfortunately, few cybersecurity solutions are sophisticated enough to protect companies from this type of attack, and most companies are unaware when their security has been compromised. Thankfully, there’s Traceable, the surefire defense against API hacks. The next-gen SaaS platform tracks all APIs and their data for an entire organization, quickly recognizing when an API’s security has been breached. Unlike competitors, Traceable analyzes user behavior and business logic in real-time to ensure that sensitive information remains uncompromised. While the battle to ensure cybersecurity is a relentless one, we believe the Traceable team is in the ideal position to adapt and pioneer new solutions to safeguard enterprise data.
The most popular way to listen to real radio from around the world
A top real-time social media and information network that connects people to the latest information about what they find interesting
Bringing transportation for everyone, everywhere

UiPath is the global market leader in robotic process automation (RPA). Their next-gen business automation platform is the best friend of those who dread having to complete boring, repetitive, time-consuming and soul-crushing tasks at work, like putting a cover page on a TPS report. With the help of software-based robots, professionals are becoming unshackled by the burden of grunt work so they can work on higher-output activities. UiPath also leverages machine learning and artificial intelligence to increase the impact of its platform, accelerating their progress to becoming the world’s preeminent company in high-tech enterprise automation.

Privileged access is foundational to enterprise security, yet most systems were built for an earlier era of infrastructure. As identities multiply across cloud, SaaS, and autonomous systems, access must become dynamic, contextual, and ephemeral by default. We believe Venice is redefining privileged access from first principles — enabling security that scales with modern infrastructure without slowing the business down.
Vercel enables the world to ship the best products.

Every year, billions and billions of dollars are lost due to identity theft and fraud. Unfortunately, fraudsters are developing increasingly sophisticated methods for bypassing standard identity verification tools, and needs for identity verification only continue to expand. Companies rely on identity verification products for everything from powering telemedicine and handling account recovery to onboarding new employees and issuing vaccination records. So how do we really verify that users are who they say they are? Estonian company Veriff is answering this critical call. The tech startup offers the world’s most advanced AI-powered identity verification platform, and is trusted by global companies like Wise, Blockchain.com, Uphold, GoUrban, and Turo. Veriff’s next-gen differentiators are its video-based technology, high rates of automation, and developer-friendly platform. So far, Veriff is only scratching the surface of the potential market opportunity, and we’re confident they will continue to grow and blaze a trail for a new era of security and legitimacy on the internet.

Credit and debit cards remain the dominant method to pay for goods and services in the Western world, but their age is showing. Card payments take 1-3 days to settle, involve significant fraud and chargeback risk, and are expensive. A new global payment infrastructure, beneficial to both merchants and consumers, is emerging. Real-time payment systems allow banks to move money directly from the accounts of senders into receivers’ and settle in seconds instead of days. Volt is leading this overdue evolution toward an instant, frictionless account-to-account future. The opportunity to disrupt how we pay – with a next-generation payment network that is global, direct and real-time – is the reason we are committed to partnering with founders Tom Greenwood, Jordan Lawrence and Steffen Vollert.
leadership: Tom Greenwood, Jordan Lawrence, Steffen Vollert

description: A push-to-talk service that allows users to instantly send voice, text, and photos to individuals and groups in real time
Transforming the business of content throughout the global entertainment ecosystem

WHOOP is a consumer brand focused on unlocking human performance. The company offers the most advanced fitness and health wearable on the market, helping users undertake behavioral change based on recovery, sleep, training and health monitoring that includes personalized recommendations and coaching feedback. By providing an up-to-date body performance report that is at once granular and holistic, WHOOP empowers members to take ownership of their personal health and wellness. It’s not just aspirational fluff, either; while using WHOOP, members experience sustained, incremental improvements across four key health indicators: resting heart rate, heart rate variability, sleep consistency, and time asleep. By capitalizing on market demand, launching new software features, and developing new products, WHOOP is quickly becoming a major brand at the cross-section of fitness, health and technology.

When we send money from one account to another, exchanging currencies along the way, we usually get shortchanged. The most notorious method for transferring money internationally is the airport currency counter, but doing it through the bank is nearly as expensive, with exorbitant exchange rates and other fees costing consumers as much as 5% of the transaction value. The worst part is that patrons are left in the dark about how much they’re losing because fees are often hidden in the exchange rates. Wise (formerly TransferWise) offers a much better alternative. The fast-growing online service uses only real exchange rates (the ones you see on Google) and offers low, transparent fees—often 80% to 90% less than traditional banks. Wise has created an entirely new infrastructure for sending payments globally, building direct connections between banks so that transfers are both cheaper and faster. The fintech company’s product is also evolving rapidly, with services for businesses and multi-currency borderless accounts for frequent travelers. Since launching in 2011, Wise has been revolutionizing the way we send and receive money internationally, creating a system perfectly suited to the interconnected world of today.

Enterprise software is entering an agentic era. AI agents are beginning to replace repetitive workflows and directly handle customer and employee interactions. With the $400B global customer experience industry as its starting point, Wonderful is developing localized AI agents tailored for non-English markets, where language fluency and regulatory nuances make automation more challenging, yet also more valuable. As AI agents form a new layer of enterprise software infrastructure, Wonderful is positioned to build the world’s AI agent platform for the non-English enterprise.

As data volumes continue to explode and data analytics have evolved from a nice-to-have to a business-critical application for decision-making, enterprises need high-performance, flexible and reliable data warehouses more than ever. That’s why top companies in every major vertical have turned to Yellowbrick Data, a data warehouse solution specifically built for the analytic needs of the world’s largest enterprises. Today’s high-tech analytics solutions need to have the quality, concurrency, compatibility, support and ecosystem integration enterprises expect, and outperform incumbents on both price and performance to boot. With this in mind, Yellowbrick built a standards-based, full-stack enterprise data warehouse that takes advantage of the capacity, speed and concurrency benefits of flash technologies. As a result, their customers experience radical improvements in analytic performance along with significantly reduced operating costs. We’re thrilled to be on this journey on the Yellowbrick road to greatness!
The local online advertising company focused on making business listings simple
A leading platform that builds social marketing and rights management software for online video

Before the dawn of Zendesk, customer service was in dire need of more Zen. Since 2007, Zendesk has been providing just that, with pioneering B2B software that helps organizations engage with their customers in ways that foster long-term customer happiness and retention. Their customer service and sales products are beautifully simple and omni-channel, allowing organizations to interact seamlessly with their customers and clients over email, phone, chat, social media and more. Backed by a user base that absolutely loves their products, Zendesk's popular SaaS platform will continue to grow its reach far and wide.

Zenefits is a next-generation HR software provider that gives growing companies a solution to easily manage payroll, benefits, compliance and more through a single online dashboard. Previously, HR software was only available to massive companies who had the resources to purchase expensive, enterprise systems from vendors like Workday, SAP and Oracle. By contrast, the extremely affordable Zenefits has made powerful but streamlined HR software accessible for the millions of U.S. companies with fewer than 1,000 employees. The SaaS application is amazingly simple and eliminates the paperwork and hassle of traditional HR management, providing better service and automated tools to create an experience that users love.

Zerto is the world’s leading B2B provider of disaster recovery, ransomware resilience and workload mobility software for both on-premises and cloud environments. When pre-existing disaster recovery solutions were rendered unworkable by the paradigm shift to virtualization and hybrid cloud deployments, Zerto swiftly responded to solve a complex task previously constrained by physical technologies. Zerto moves data/application protection and replication into the virtual layer, working “across the aisle” to connect disparate technologies for the benefit of IT and business leaders. Additionally, Zerto provides the ability to build infrastructures using private, hybrid or public clouds to help IT organizations become more agile in supporting changing business needs. The result is unparalleled flexibility, scalability, and protection of mission critical infrastructure.

While the recruiting industry began its transition from offline to online almost as soon as the Internet was commercialized, the market is large and small- and medium-sized businesses have remained largely underserved—until now. ZipRecruiter’s pioneering B2B suite of hiring products and subscription plans deliver a better way to recruit and hire for companies of all sizes, including the SMB segment that makes up roughly half of the US workforce. Fast and easy to use, the successful HR hiring platform helps employers distribute and manage job postings across the web. By providing an efficient and flexible subscription product, ZipRecruiter makes job distribution and candidate management simple for companies that may otherwise find enterprise-class software cumbersome and expensive. Meanwhile, candidates benefit from ZipRecruiter’s proprietary matching technology, which greatly simplifies and expedites the process of finding a new job.
